source_record.title: High-risk genomic consensus validation for patients with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma using next-generation sequencing.
source_record.study_context: Blood validation study of high-risk genomic consensus staging using next-generation sequencing in newly diagnosed multiple myeloma and first-relapse contexts. This public extraction uses the source as high-risk disease-context support only.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source validates a high-risk genomic consensus framework in a large cohort context and reports high-risk classification in newly diagnosed and first-relapse samples, supporting unmet high-risk context as a disease-context modifier while preserving genomic method, cohort, disease timing, and non-prognostic public-artifact boundaries.
limitations: Cohort-level validation source; not a patient-specific prognosis tool.
Does not establish treatment selection, trial eligibility, monitoring guidance, or therapy fit.
High-risk context is a modifier in this geometry, not a causal mechanism by itself.
Requires aligned genomic definitions and disease timing before comparison with other high-risk sources.

what_this_supports: The unmet-high-risk-context-v0 branch can move to covered-for-v0-navigation as public artifact coverage because it now has one review-level disease-context source and one cohort-scale genomic validation source.
The geometry should preserve high-risk context as a modifier that affects interpretation needs, not as a treatment selector.
High-risk context should remain linked to genomic method, disease timing, cohort definition, and uncertainty.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not predict prognosis for any person.
It does not recommend treatment, trial enrollment, monitoring, transplant, or product selection.
It does not rank high-risk features or substitute for clinical genomic interpretation.
It does not validate clinical actionability or cure claims.

review_notes: Created for Phase 6 falsification and hypothesis generation.
Source details checked against PubMed metadata for PMID 40991836.
The extraction intentionally keeps high-risk context non-prognostic and non-actionable.
